title: Representative workflow/BMAD planning-to-execution parity fixture

representativePath.summary: One deterministic milestone/slice/task planning-to-execution loop that moves from plan creation through task completion and emits tracked artifacts plus persisted state transitions.
representativePath.whyThisPathMatters: It is the narrowest representative workflow path that proves umb can structure work, advance it through concrete execution state, and surface operator-visible artifacts without ambient human input.
representativePath.bmadRelevance: The contract scopes workflow/BMAD parity to structured planning and implementation-phase progression rather than claiming full coverage of every BMAD pipeline.
inputs: Task completion payload with verification evidence

phases.expectedStatus: milestone and slice rows persist as pending planning state
slice goal and task rows persist and render deterministic plan artifacts
task completion persists verification evidence, toggles the task plan checkbox, and renders the task summary artifact

requiredStateAssertions.query: milestone row exists with title, vision, and status
slice row exists with goal and task rows are present
task row stores oneLiner, narrative, verification_result, blocker_discovered, and verification evidence

failureDiagnostics.summary: The parity lane must identify which expected artifact path was missing or malformed.
The parity lane must identify which entity field failed to transition to the expected persisted value.
failureDiagnostics.operatorSurface: entity, field, expected value, observed value

failureDiagnostics.summary: The parity lane must identify when task completion omitted the verification evidence rows needed for truthful closeout.

observability.artifactContract: The fixture is truthful only when artifact paths and persisted state assertions agree on the same representative loop.
observability.diagnosticExpectation: Downstream workflow parity reporting must name the missing artifact, invalid transition, or absent verification evidence directly.

notes: This fixture intentionally scopes workflow parity to one representative planning-to-execution path instead of over-claiming coverage for every workflow template or BMAD pipeline.
Later tasks may implement the replay/recording in repo or installed mode, but they must preserve this artifact and state-transition contract.
